Notes: Ad-hoc UAT for the Set 045 dual-primary log-harvest surface: dabbler-launch wrapper, Claude+Copilot per-event parsers, narration template regeneration, and the Session Set Explorer signal-badge + conflict-pill rendering shipped in S5. Schema notes: each item carries `Passes` (canonical truth used by the UAT Checklist Editor app) and `Result` (set to 'pending' for the Dabbler extension's pending-count parser). Update both fields together when marking an item complete.

Review.ItemLabel: Writes launch record for Claude backend
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.dabbler_launch --backend claude --workspace-cwd $PWD --dry-run` from a workspace dir.
Review.Expectation: A JSON launch record is printed to stdout with engine='claude', workspace_cwd matching, raw_ref.launch_id present (uuid4), event_type='launch'. Exit 0.

Review.ItemLabel: Writes launch record for Copilot backend
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.dabbler_launch --backend copilot --workspace-cwd $PWD --dry-run`.
Review.Expectation: Same shape as Claude case but engine='copilot'. Exit 0.

Review.ItemLabel: Refuses unknown backend
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.dabbler_launch --backend gemini --workspace-cwd $PWD --dry-run`.
Review.Expectation: Exit non-zero with a clear error message naming the supported backends. No launch record emitted.

Review.ItemLabel: Produces JSON coverage per session-set
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.joiner --coverage --json` from the repo root.
Review.Expectation: Stdout is a parseable JSON array. Each entry has fields per joiner-spec.md §6 CoverageSummary (set_slug, wrapper_present, native_present, narration_present, bypass_present). Set 045 row shows narration_present=true (S4 templates exist).

Review.ItemLabel: Produces JSON conflict list
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -m ai_router.joiner --conflicts --json`.
Review.Expectation: Stdout is a parseable JSON array. Each entry has fields per joiner-spec.md §3.5 ConflictReport (set_slug, kind, severity, note). Kinds match the locked enum (engine-mismatch / bare-touch / stale-checkout-touch / writer-bypass).

Review.ItemLabel: Auto-selects single in-progress set
Review.HumanAction: With exactly one session set in 'in-progress' status in the workspace, run `Dabbler: Regenerate Narration Templates` from the Command Palette.
Review.Expectation: No quickpick appears; the command proceeds directly. A `Generating narration templates…` progress notification shows briefly. On success, a toast offers 'Open Rendered CLAUDE.md' and 'Copy to consumer workspace…' actions.

Review.ItemLabel: Quickpicks when multiple sets in-progress
Review.HumanAction: With two or more session sets in 'in-progress' status, run the command.
Review.Expectation: A quickpick lists the in-progress sets. Selecting one proceeds to render; cancelling exits silently.

Review.ItemLabel: Writes both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
Review.HumanAction: After a successful regen, inspect `<set>/narration-templates/`.
Review.Expectation: Both `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` exist. Both contain a `[DABBLER-NARRATION v1 phase=session-start set=<slug> session=<N> total=<T> effort=<E>]` marker that matches the current `session-state.json` values.

Review.ItemLabel: Marker passes detect_marker validation
Review.HumanAction: Run `python -c "from ai_router.narration import detect_marker; import sys; print(detect_marker(open(sys.argv[1]).read()))" docs/session-sets/<slug>/narration-templates/CLAUDE.md`.
Review.Expectation: Output is a non-None Marker tuple/dict with phase='session-start', set=<slug>, session=<N>, total=<T>, effort=<E>; no `flags` indicating a semantic-check failure.

Review.ItemLabel: Folder picker + overwrite confirm flow
Review.HumanAction: After regen, click 'Copy to consumer workspace…' in the success toast. Pick a target folder. If the destination CLAUDE.md exists, accept the overwrite confirm.
Review.Expectation: Both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md land in the picked folder. Overwrite confirm fires only when a target file exists; cancel preserves the existing file.

Review.ItemLabel: Lights when a wrapper launch record exists
Review.HumanAction: Invoke `dabbler-launch` once against an in-progress set; refresh the Session Sets view.
Review.Expectation: The set's row shows a 'W' badge in the harvest-signals strip. Tooltip explains 'wrapper launch record present'.

Review.ItemLabel: Lights when joiner finds native-log events
Review.HumanAction: For a set that has been worked on with Claude Code or Copilot CLI in this workspace, refresh the view.
Review.Expectation: Row shows 'N' badge. Tooltip names which backend's logs were found.

Review.ItemLabel: Lights when a narration marker was emitted
Review.HumanAction: Have an assistant session emit the v1.1 marker (use a CLAUDE.md from the narration-templates folder), then refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows 'M' badge. Tooltip 'narration marker detected in <backend> log'.

Review.ItemLabel: Lights when writer-bypass writes are detected
Review.HumanAction: Make a hand-edit to a session-state.json that bypasses register_session_start / close_session, then refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows 'B' badge. Tooltip 'writer-bypass file activity detected ±2s outside the close-out window'.

Review.ItemLabel: No badges render for a not-started set
Review.HumanAction: Inspect a freshly-created set with no wrapper / native-log / marker / bypass activity.
Review.Expectation: Harvest-signals strip is absent or fully greyed; no W/N/M/B badges light.

Review.ItemLabel: Badges remain legible at simulated colorblind palettes
Review.HumanAction: Open the view at default zoom + at 200% zoom; mentally simulate or use a colorblind-simulation extension across deuteranopia/protanopia/tritanopia.
Review.Expectation: Badges remain distinguishable from one another at all three simulations (IBM colorblind-safe palette per memory `gauges_sizing_followup`).

Review.ItemLabel: Renders when joiner reports it
Review.HumanAction: Set up state where the orchestrator block records engine='claude' but the joiner sees a different engine's native log emissions; refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows an 'engine-mismatch' conflict pill with severity color; tooltip explains the mismatch.

Review.ItemLabel: Renders for unbound native activity
Review.HumanAction: Use a free-running assistant against a set that has no wrapper launch and no narration marker; refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows a 'bare-touch' conflict pill; tooltip mentions correlation gap.

Review.ItemLabel: Renders for activity past stale window
Review.HumanAction: Manually backdate `orchestrator.lastActivityAt` >2h in a session-state.json; refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows a 'stale-checkout-touch' conflict pill; tooltip names the stale window.

Review.ItemLabel: Renders for file activity outside close-out window
Review.HumanAction: Make a hand-edit to a session-state.json or events ledger outside the ±2s close-out window; refresh.
Review.Expectation: Row shows a 'writer-bypass' conflict pill; tooltip explains the bypass detection.

Review.ItemLabel: Multiple conflicts on one row each render individually
Review.HumanAction: Construct a set with two distinct conflicts; refresh.
Review.Expectation: Both pills render side-by-side; neither suppresses the other; CSS layout does not overflow the row.

Review.ItemLabel: Pill indent scales with font-size
Review.HumanAction: Set the editor's font-size to 11px, then 14px, then 18px; verify pill indent re-flows.
Review.Expectation: Pills remain aligned to the row content at every font-size (S5 Round-A fix: CSS custom property + calc() rather than hard-coded 60px indent).

Review.ItemLabel: Shows when dabbler-ai-router is not installed
Review.HumanAction: Uninstall dabbler-ai-router from the active venv (`pip uninstall dabbler-ai-router`); reload the VS Code window; open the Session Sets view.
Review.Expectation: A one-time info toast appears: explains that the harvest surface needs `pip install dabbler-ai-router`; offers an 'Open settings' action that navigates to the dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath setting.

Review.ItemLabel: Toast does not re-show
Review.HumanAction: After dismissing the toast, refresh the view several times.
Review.Expectation: No additional toasts surface. The Explorer continues to render every row without harvest badges / conflict pills (graceful degrade).

Review.ItemLabel: Re-installs and the next refresh restores badges
Review.HumanAction: Run `pip install dabbler-ai-router>=0.8.0`; reload the VS Code window; open the view.
Review.Expectation: Badges and conflict pills render again on next refresh; no leftover degraded-state UI.

Review.ItemLabel: docs/narration-templates.md is operator-accurate
Review.HumanAction: Read docs/narration-templates.md end-to-end. Walk through the 'Generating a template' section step-by-step.
Review.Expectation: Every command and file path in the doc matches reality. No dangling references; no commands that error out.

Review.ItemLabel: Cross-repo harvest notice is paste-ready
Review.HumanAction: Paste the cross-repo notice snippet into a scratch CLAUDE.md (in any consumer repo); read it as if you were a fresh orchestrator landing in that repo.
Review.Expectation: The snippet is self-contained, links resolve, and the 'how to enable' steps are clear without needing the source repo as context.

Review.ItemLabel: Rebuild-trap note prevents the stale-bundle false-positive
Review.HumanAction: Read CONTRIBUTING.md > Layer 3 section. Try invoking `npx playwright test` directly (without `npm run compile` first) and observe the failure mode.
Review.Expectation: The CONTRIBUTING note correctly warns against bare `npx playwright test`; following `npm run test:playwright` succeeds when the code is in fact passing.
